fields.name: addendum_or_appendix_name
fields.std_description: The name of an attached addendum, appendix, exhibit, or supplemental document referenced with the franchise agreement.

fields.name: agreement_title
fields.std_description: The formal name of the franchise contract shown in the document heading or title block.

fields.name: effective_date
fields.std_description: The date the franchise agreement is made effective or entered into.

fields.name: exclusivity
fields.std_description: Whether the franchise grant is exclusive or non-exclusive within a territory.

fields.name: franchisee_name
fields.std_description: The legal name of the franchisee, master franchisee, or grantee party to the agreement.

fields.name: franchisor_name
fields.std_description: The legal name of the franchisor or grantor party to the agreement.

fields.name: guarantor_name
fields.std_description: The name of a person or entity providing a guaranty for franchise obligations.

fields.name: initial_franchise_fee
fields.std_description: The upfront fee payable when entering the franchise agreement.

fields.name: renewal_right
fields.std_description: Whether the agreement provides for renewal or options to renew the franchise term.

fields.name: table_of_contents_section
fields.std_description: A section heading appearing in the agreement's table of contents, often indicating the contract's major topics.

fields.name: term
fields.std_description: The initial or overall duration of the franchise relationship stated in the agreement.

fields.name: territory
fields.std_description: The geographic area in which the franchise rights are granted or discussed.

fields.name: accredited_investor_requirement
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the offering is limited to accredited investors or includes an accredited-investor certification requirement.

fields.name: agent_entity
fields.std_description: The broker-dealer, sales agent, or other intermediary appointed to assist with the offering.

fields.name: agent_party
fields.rlm_description: The agent, broker-dealer, or intermediary engaged in the offering or resale process.

fields.name: agreement_date
fields.std_description: The effective or execution date stated for the agreement or related exhibit.

fields.name: agreement_title
fields.std_description: The formal title or type of the agreement governing the offering or related transaction.

fields.name: closing_date
fields.rlm_description: The date or time when the offering or transaction closes.

fields.name: compensation_options_percentage
fields.std_description: The percentage of sold offered shares used to calculate compensation options or similar agent compensation.

fields.name: investor_eligibility
fields.rlm_description: The class or status required for permitted purchasers or offerees.

fields.name: issuer_entity
fields.std_description: The primary issuer, company, or corporation conducting the offering or transaction.

fields.name: jurisdiction
fields.rlm_description: The jurisdiction or law governing the offer, sale, or compliance obligation.

fields.name: material_adverse_effect
fields.rlm_description: Whether a material adverse effect or similar material adverse change threshold applies.

fields.name: notice_period
fields.rlm_description: The number of days required for advance notice or timing before an action.

fields.name: offered_security_type
fields.std_description: The class or type of security being offered or sold under the agreement.

fields.name: offered_shares_amount
fields.std_description: The number of shares or units authorized or proposed to be offered, including any stated increase amount.

fields.name: offering_amount
fields.rlm_description: The number of securities, shares, or allocation quantity referenced in the clause.

fields.name: offering_date
fields.rlm_description: The date the agreement or offering becomes effective or is referenced as the start date.

fields.name: offering_jurisdiction
fields.std_description: The geographic market or jurisdiction where the securities are offered or sold.

fields.name: offering_party
fields.rlm_description: The issuer, company, trust, or other party whose securities are being offered or sold.

fields.name: offering_price
fields.std_description: The stated subscription or offering price per share or unit.

fields.name: offering_type
fields.std_description: The distribution or sale structure used for the offering, such as subscription, community, private placement, or orderly marketing.

fields.name: payment_terms
fields.rlm_description: The fee, commission, payment, or reimbursement amount referenced in the clause.

fields.name: prospectus_document
fields.rlm_description: The prospectus or related offering document used for the transaction.

fields.name: regulation_reference
fields.std_description: The securities law, rule, or regulatory framework referenced for compliance or exemption purposes.

fields.name: regulatory_body
fields.rlm_description: The regulator or self-regulatory body with approval, filing, or oversight authority.

fields.name: regulatory_compliance
fields.rlm_description: Whether required regulatory registration, filing, or approval conditions have been satisfied.

fields.name: security_type
fields.rlm_description: The class or series of securities subject to the clause.

fields.name: solicitation_restriction
fields.rlm_description: Whether prohibited solicitation, advertising, or selling efforts are restricted or absent.

fields.name: administrator_name
fields.std_description: The administrator, servicing agent, or similar operational service provider named in the agreement.

fields.name: adviser_name
fields.std_description: The investment adviser or adviser entity referenced in the agreement.

fields.name: agreement_date
fields.rlm_description: The agreement date referenced in the clause.

fields.name: agreement_effective_date
fields.std_description: The date on which the agreement is stated to be effective or made as of.

fields.name: agreement_title
fields.std_description: The formal title or heading of the agreement document.
fields.rlm_description: The agreement title referenced in the clause.

fields.name: agreement_type
fields.std_description: The category of contract reflected by the document heading or body.
fields.rlm_description: The agreement type referenced in the clause.

fields.name: distributor_name
fields.std_description: The distributor or principal underwriter named in the agreement.
fields.rlm_description: The distributor name referenced in the clause.

fields.name: effective_date
fields.rlm_description: A date on which a contract or agreement is stated to be dated as of.

fields.name: fund_name
fields.std_description: The fund, trust, or series identified as the subject of the servicing arrangement.
fields.rlm_description: The fund name referenced in the clause.

fields.name: funds_listed
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the agreement covers multiple named funds or series rather than a single fund.

fields.name: party_1_name
fields.std_description: The first named party to the agreement, typically the trust, adviser, issuer, or company.

fields.name: party_2_name
fields.std_description: The second named party to the agreement, typically the adviser, administrator, distributor, or servicing agent.

fields.name: party_role
fields.rlm_description: The party role referenced in the clause.

fields.name: service_provider_role
fields.std_description: The functional role assigned to the counterparty providing services under the agreement.

fields.name: trust_name
fields.std_description: The trust or statutory trust acting on behalf of one or more funds.
fields.rlm_description: The trust entity referenced as issuer, holder of assets, or contract party.

fields.name: acknowledgment_of_receipt
fields.std_description: The clause acknowledging receipt and sufficiency or adequacy of consideration.

fields.name: agreement_action_phrase
fields.rlm_description: The phrase stating the parties' formal action or agreement, such as covenanted, agreed, or entered into.

fields.name: agreement_binding_intent
fields.rlm_description: Whether the opening clause expressly states an intent to be legally bound.

fields.name: agreement_heading
fields.rlm_description: The document heading or opening article/part heading that begins the substantive agreement section.

fields.name: agreement_intro_phrase
fields.rlm_description: The standardized opening phrase introducing the agreement recital and operative clauses.

fields.name: binding_intro_language
fields.rlm_description: The operative transition stating that the parties covenant, agree, or are legally bound to the agreement terms.

fields.name: consideration_acknowledgment
fields.rlm_description: The acknowledgment that receipt and sufficiency of consideration are recognized by the parties.

fields.name: consideration_clause
fields.std_description: The consideration language stating what is being exchanged or acknowledged as consideration for the agreement.

fields.name: consideration_phrase
fields.rlm_description: The clause language describing the consideration supporting the agreement, often mentioning mutual covenants or good and valuable consideration.

fields.name: defined_term
fields.rlm_description: A defined term introduced in the agreement's definitions or interpretation section.

fields.name: definition_reference
fields.rlm_description: The cross-reference or location where a defined term is explained.

fields.name: document_heading
fields.rlm_description: The heading or title line used to introduce the agreement's structure or first substantive part.

fields.name: introductory_recital_text
fields.rlm_description: The standard introductory recital language explaining the background, premises, or consideration for the agreement.

fields.name: legal_binding_intent
fields.std_description: Whether the recital expressly states that the parties intend to be legally bound.

fields.name: mutual_covenants_reference
fields.std_description: Reference to mutual covenants, promises, representations, warranties, or agreements as part of the consideration or basis for the contract.

fields.name: opening_section_label
fields.std_description: The first heading or section label that follows the recital and opening language.

fields.name: opening_section_number
fields.rlm_description: The number or identifier of the first substantive section following the recital or opening language.

fields.name: opening_section_title
fields.std_description: The title of the first substantive section or chapter immediately following the opening language.
fields.rlm_description: The title of the first substantive section immediately following the opening recital.

fields.name: opening_transition_phrase
fields.std_description: The standard introductory recital phrase that transitions into the operative agreement language.

fields.name: party_agreement_intro
fields.std_description: The operative phrase introducing the parties' agreement to the following terms.

fields.name: party_reference
fields.rlm_description: The parties or party names referenced in the opening recital or introductory clause.

fields.name: recitals_reference
fields.std_description: Language incorporating or referring to prior recitals or foregoing premises as part of the agreement basis.

fields.name: referenced_agreement_name
fields.rlm_description: The title or name of the agreement being referenced in the opening clause.

fields.name: automatic_renewal
fields.std_description: Whether the agreement renews automatically absent notice or other action.

fields.name: early_termination_triggers
fields.rlm_description: Events or circumstances that permit early termination before the stated end of term.

fields.name: effective_date
fields.std_description: The date on which the agreement becomes effective or commences.
fields.rlm_description: Date the agreement or term becomes effective or commences.

fields.name: extension_period
fields.std_description: The additional period for which the agreement may be extended beyond the stated term.

fields.name: initial_term_length
fields.std_description: The length of the first contractual term before any renewal.

fields.name: mutual_consent_termination
fields.rlm_description: Whether the agreement can be terminated by mutual written consent.

fields.name: mutual_written_agreement_required
fields.std_description: Whether extension, renewal, or changes require mutual written agreement of both parties.

fields.name: non_renewal_notice_period
fields.std_description: The advance notice period required to prevent automatic renewal or extension.

fields.name: renewal_automatically
fields.rlm_description: Whether the agreement renews automatically at the end of each term.

fields.name: renewal_frequency
fields.rlm_description: How often the agreement renews or extends.

fields.name: renewal_notice_period_days
fields.rlm_description: Number of days of prior notice required to stop renewal or provide non-renewal notice.

fields.name: renewal_term_length
fields.std_description: The length of each renewal period after the initial term.
fields.rlm_description: Length of each renewal or extension term.

fields.name: renewal_type
fields.rlm_description: Whether renewal is automatic, optional, or by mutual agreement.

fields.name: survival_of_obligations
fields.std_description: Whether rights or obligations survive expiration or earlier termination of the agreement.

fields.name: survival_of_rights_and_obligations
fields.rlm_description: Whether rights or obligations survive expiration or termination.

fields.name: term_end_date
fields.std_description: A fixed date on which the agreement or term expires or ends.
fields.rlm_description: Date the term or initial term expires or ends.

fields.name: term_end_event
fields.rlm_description: Event that ends the term when the agreement does not use a fixed end date.

fields.name: term_length
fields.rlm_description: Length of the agreement term or initial term.

fields.name: term_start_event
fields.rlm_description: Event that starts the term when the agreement does not use a fixed start date.

fields.name: termination_cure_period_days
fields.rlm_description: Cure period after breach notice before termination becomes effective.

fields.name: termination_notice_period
fields.std_description: The notice period required to terminate the agreement or exercise a termination right.

fields.name: termination_notice_period_days
fields.rlm_description: Number of days of notice required to terminate the agreement.

fields.name: termination_right
fields.std_description: Whether a party may terminate the agreement by notice or upon specified conditions.

fields.name: affected_party
fields.rlm_description: The party whose performance is delayed, hindered, or excused by the force majeure event.

fields.name: excluded_events
fields.std_description: Events expressly carved out from the force majeure definition or stated not to qualify as force majeure.
fields.rlm_description: Events specifically excluded from force majeure treatment, often including payment obligations, financial distress, or self-inflicted events.

fields.name: excused_obligations
fields.rlm_description: The contractual obligations or performance duties that are suspended, excused, or delayed during force majeure.

fields.name: force_majeure_definition
fields.std_description: The contractual definition of what qualifies as a force majeure event, typically framed as events beyond a party's reasonable control or not reasonably foreseeable/avoidable.

fields.name: force_majeure_duration
fields.rlm_description: The length of time the force majeure condition must continue before additional remedies become available.

fields.name: force_majeure_event
fields.rlm_description: The event or circumstance that qualifies as force majeure and excuses or limits performance.

fields.name: force_majeure_event_list
fields.std_description: The enumerated examples of events that are expressly included as force majeure events under the clause.

fields.name: foreseeability_requirement
fields.rlm_description: Whether the event was unforeseeable to the affected party.

fields.name: liability_excuse
fields.std_description: Whether the clause relieves the affected party from liability for non-performance or delay caused by force majeure.

fields.name: mitigation_requirement
fields.rlm_description: Whether the affected party must use reasonable efforts or due diligence to avoid or mitigate the event and its effects.

fields.name: non_affected_party
fields.rlm_description: The counterparty receiving notice or whose obligations are correspondingly affected by the force majeure event.

fields.name: notice_content
fields.rlm_description: The information that must be included in a force majeure notice, such as particulars, certification, or supporting facts.

fields.name: notice_period
fields.rlm_description: The deadline for giving force majeure notice after the event occurs.

fields.name: notice_requirement
fields.std_description: The required notice timing or method a party must use to invoke force majeure or report the event.
fields.rlm_description: The obligation to give notice to the other party when a force majeure event occurs, often with specified timing and content requirements.

fields.name: payment_obligation_excluded
fields.std_description: Whether payment obligations are expressly excluded from suspension or force majeure relief.

fields.name: performance_suspension
fields.std_description: Whether the affected party's performance is suspended during the force majeure period.

fields.name: qualifying_event_categories
fields.rlm_description: Categories of events that may trigger force majeure, such as natural disasters, war, government action, labor disruptions, or utility failures.

fields.name: reasonable_control_standard
fields.std_description: The standard used to determine whether the event is outside the affected party's control, often tied to reasonable control, due diligence, or inability to prevent/overcome the event.
fields.rlm_description: Whether the event was outside the affected party's reasonable control.

fields.name: supporting_documentation
fields.std_description: Any required certificate, report, or other documentary evidence that must be provided to substantiate the force majeure event.

fields.name: suspension_or_extension_right
fields.rlm_description: Whether the affected party may suspend performance, extend the term, or otherwise adjust obligations during the force majeure period.

fields.name: termination_right
fields.std_description: Whether the clause gives a party the right to terminate the agreement or affected services if the force majeure event continues beyond a stated period.
fields.rlm_description: Whether a party may terminate the agreement if the force majeure condition continues beyond a stated period.

fields.name: termination_threshold
fields.std_description: The period of continued force majeure after which termination rights arise.

fields.name: agreement_definition
fields.rlm_description: Describes what documents, amendments, schedules, or instruments are included within the defined term Agreement.

fields.name: amend_and_restate_language
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause states that an original agreement is amended and restated by the current agreement.

fields.name: amendment_requires_writing
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause requires written execution for modifications or amendments.

fields.name: conflict_priority_rule
fields.std_description: Specifies which document controls when the agreement conflicts with an exhibit, schedule, amendment, or body provision.
fields.rlm_description: States which document, exhibit, or provision controls if there is a conflict among related documents.

fields.name: effective_date_reference
fields.rlm_description: Identifies a date from which the agreement or a referenced understanding is effective or operative.

fields.name: entire_agreement_statement
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause expressly states that the agreement is the entire or complete agreement between the parties.
fields.rlm_description: States that the agreement is the complete and exclusive understanding of the parties and supersedes prior agreements or understandings.

fields.name: incorporated_documents_reference
fields.rlm_description: References exhibits, schedules, appendices, or other documents incorporated into the agreement by reference.

fields.name: incorporates_schedules_exhibits
fields.std_description: Indicates whether schedules, exhibits, appendices, or attachments are incorporated into and form part of the agreement.

fields.name: modification_requirement
fields.rlm_description: Specifies the formal requirement for amending, waiving, or modifying the agreement.

fields.name: oral_and_written_coverage
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause expressly includes both oral and written prior agreements or understandings in its supersession language.

fields.name: prior_or_contemporaneous_coverage
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause expressly covers both prior and contemporaneous agreements or understandings.

fields.name: subject_matter_scope
fields.std_description: Describes the subject matter or scope to which the entire-agreement effect applies.
fields.rlm_description: Defines the subject matter or transaction scope to which the entire-agreement clause applies.

fields.name: superseded_prior_materials
fields.std_description: Lists the categories of prior materials or communications expressly superseded by the agreement.

fields.name: supersedes_prior_agreements
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause says the agreement supersedes prior agreements, understandings, or discussions.
fields.rlm_description: Identifies prior agreements, understandings, negotiations, or representations that are superseded or replaced.

fields.name: decision_rule
fields.std_description: The voting or approval standard used for committee action or decisions.

fields.name: equal_representation
fields.std_description: Whether the committee is structured with equal representation from each party.

fields.name: meeting_format

fields.rlm_description: The date on which the agreement is executed or dated.

fields.std_description: The geographic scope in which rights apply, including any carve-outs or later exclusions.

fields.name: beta_exclusion
fields.rlm_description: Indicates whether beta versions are excluded from the launch definition.

fields.name: effective_date
fields.std_description: The agreement effective date used as a reference point for launch-related timing.
fields.rlm_description: The date on which the agreement or defined period becomes effective.

fields.name: hosting_location
fields.rlm_description: The location or environment where the service or infrastructure is hosted.

fields.name: hosting_party
fields.rlm_description: The party responsible for hosting or operating the relevant service or infrastructure.

fields.name: launch_condition
fields.rlm_description: A condition that must be satisfied before the launch date is deemed to occur.

fields.name: launch_date
fields.rlm_description: The date on which a product, site, or service is launched, becomes operational, or is first made generally available.

fields.name: launch_date_beta_exclusion
fields.std_description: Whether beta versions are excluded from the launch-date definition.

fields.name: launch_date_defined_event
fields.rlm_description: The defined event or milestone used to establish the launch date.

fields.name: launch_date_expected_date
fields.std_description: An anticipated or estimated launch date stated in the agreement.

fields.name: launch_date_expected_value
fields.rlm_description: A specified or expected calendar date associated with the launch definition.

fields.name: launch_date_product
fields.std_description: The product, service, or site whose launch is being defined.

fields.name: launch_date_reference_date
fields.rlm_description: A reference date used as the anchor for a launch period or launch-related calculation.

fields.name: launch_date_selector
fields.rlm_description: The party, person, or mechanism responsible for selecting or determining the launch date.

fields.name: launch_date_term
fields.std_description: The defined contractual term used to label the launch date concept.

fields.name: launch_date_trigger
fields.std_description: The event or condition that causes the launch date to occur.

fields.name: launch_date_trigger_event
fields.rlm_description: The event or condition that triggers or determines the launch date.

fields.name: launch_period_end
fields.std_description: The end date of a defined launch period.
fields.rlm_description: The ending date of a defined launch period.

fields.name: launch_period_start
fields.std_description: The start date of a defined launch period.
fields.rlm_description: The starting date of a defined launch period.

fields.name: launch_reference_event
fields.std_description: The specific operational milestone used as the launch reference event.

fields.name: product_or_service_name
fields.rlm_description: The named product, software, site, or service whose launch date is being defined.

fields.name: public_availability_requirement
fields.rlm_description: Whether the relevant product or service must be publicly available or generally available.

fields.name: agreement_effective_date
fields.std_description: The date on which the sponsorship agreement is made effective or entered into.

fields.name: agreement_parties
fields.std_description: The named entities entering into the sponsorship agreement as contracting parties.

fields.name: agreement_term
fields.std_description: The overall period during which the sponsorship agreement remains in force.

fields.name: arbitration_required
fields.std_description: Whether disputes over fee adjustments or related matters must be resolved by arbitration.

fields.name: early_termination_window
fields.std_description: A specified date range during which one party may exercise an early termination right.

fields.name: insurance_coverage_limit
fields.std_description: The minimum liability insurance coverage required to be maintained by a party.

fields.name: late_fee_rate
fields.std_description: The interest or late charge rate applied to overdue payments.

fields.name: marks_usage_rights
fields.std_description: Rights granted to each party to use the other party's names, logos, trademarks, or marks.

fields.name: notice_period
fields.std_description: The cure or notice period required before termination or other enforcement action may occur.

fields.name: payment_amount
fields.std_description: The monetary consideration payable under the sponsorship agreement.
fields.rlm_description: A monetary amount due under the agreement.

fields.name: payment_schedule
fields.std_description: The dates or timing on which sponsorship payments are due.
fields.rlm_description: The timing or installment structure for making a payment.

fields.name: sponsorship_category
fields.std_description: The product or market category in which the sponsor is designated or licensed.

fields.name: sponsorship_rights
fields.std_description: The advertising, promotional, and related benefits granted to the sponsor under the agreement.
fields.rlm_description: The advertising, promotional, or other rights granted under the sponsorship arrangement.

fields.name: term
fields.rlm_description: The date on which the contract term begins.

fields.name: heading_disclaimer
fields.rlm_description: Indicates whether the clause states that headings, titles, captions, section headings, or similar subdivision labels are for convenience/reference only and do not affect interpretation, construction, meaning, scope, or intent of the agreement.

fields.name: heading_section_label
fields.std_description: The label or title used for the headings disclaimer provision itself.

fields.name: headings_are_for_convenience
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause states that headings are included only for convenience or reference.

fields.name: headings_as_descriptive_only
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause characterizes headings as descriptive only or for reference purposes only.

fields.name: headings_do_not_affect_interpretation
fields.rlm_description: Indicates the clause expressly states headings do not affect the meaning, interpretation, or construction of the agreement or any provision.

fields.name: headings_no_interpretive_effect
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause states that headings do not affect interpretation, construction, or meaning.

fields.name: headings_no_limit_scope
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause expressly says headings do not define, limit, or affect the scope or meaning of the agreement or provisions.

fields.name: headings_not_part_of_agreement
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause states that headings do not form part of the agreement or text.

fields.name: headings_omitted_for_construction
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the clause says the agreement should be construed as if headings were omitted.

fields.name: subdivisions_covered
fields.std_description: The types of document subdivisions expressly covered by the disclaimer, such as articles, sections, schedules, paragraphs, or clauses.

fields.name: amendment_requires_written_agreement
fields.rlm_description: Whether changes, amendments, or supplements to the agreement require a signed supplemental agreement or similar formal writing.

fields.name: applicable_law_fallback
fields.rlm_description: A secondary legal regime that applies if the primary governing law does not cover a matter.

fields.name: bilingual_equal_effect
fields.rlm_description: Whether two language versions are stated to have equal legal effect.

fields.name: compliance_with_applicable_laws
fields.rlm_description: Whether a party must comply with or warrant non-violation of applicable laws.

fields.name: conflicts_of_law_exclusion
fields.std_description: Whether the clause expressly excludes conflicts-of-law rules or similar choice-of-law principles.

fields.name: contract_language
fields.std_description: The language or languages in which the agreement is executed or prepared.

fields.name: contract_term_duration
fields.rlm_description: The stated validity or term length of the agreement.

fields.name: counterpart_count
fields.std_description: The number of original counterparts or copies executed for the agreement.

fields.name: governing_law
fields.rlm_description: The jurisdiction or legal system whose laws govern the agreement.

fields.name: governing_law_jurisdiction
fields.std_description: The jurisdiction or legal system whose laws govern the agreement or clause.

fields.name: governing_law_scope
fields.std_description: The set of contract matters covered by the governing law provision, such as validity, construction, interpretation, enforcement, or disputes.

fields.name: incoterms_reference
fields.rlm_description: A reference to Incoterms or similar trade terms incorporated into the contract.

fields.name: interpretation_standard
fields.rlm_description: The law or legal framework used to interpret the agreement, if stated separately from governing law.

fields.name: jurisdiction_or_territorial_scope
fields.rlm_description: The geographic territory in which rights, obligations, or use rights are valid or restricted.

fields.name: language_precedence
fields.std_description: Which language version prevails if there is a discrepancy between bilingual contract versions.

fields.name: language_priority
fields.rlm_description: The language version that controls if multiple language versions conflict.

fields.name: law_definition_scope
fields.rlm_description: The substances, sources, or categories included within a defined law term.

fields.name: law_definition_term
fields.rlm_description: A defined term that names the body of law referenced in the clause.

fields.name: prc_laws_reference
fields.std_description: Whether the clause expressly references PRC laws or the People's Republic of China as the applicable legal regime or source of law.

fields.name: restricted_region
fields.rlm_description: A region where use, authorization, or performance is prohibited or excluded.

fields.name: severability
fields.rlm_description: Whether an illegal, invalid, or unenforceable provision is reformed or severed so the rest remains effective.

fields.name: territorial_use_restriction
fields.std_description: Whether the clause limits use, validity, or authorization to the PRC or another specified territory.

fields.name: waiver_requires_writing
fields.rlm_description: Whether waiver of rights must be expressly made in writing.

fields.name: agreement_name
fields.rlm_description: The name or reference of the agreement.

fields.name: approved_use_mediums
fields.std_description: The media or product contexts in which the licensed mark may be used.

fields.name: benefit_inures_to
fields.rlm_description: The party that benefits from use of the licensed mark.

fields.name: consideration_acknowledged
fields.rlm_description: Indicates whether the parties acknowledge receipt and sufficiency of consideration.

fields.name: guideline_schedule_reference
fields.std_description: The schedule or attachment that contains usage guidelines or artwork specifications.

fields.name: inspection_right
fields.std_description: Whether Lucent has the right to inspect approved uses or goods bearing the licensed mark.

fields.name: license_grant_scope
fields.std_description: The specific trademark/logo and permitted uses granted under the license.

fields.name: licensed_mark_name
fields.std_description: The trademark or co-branding logo name identified for use under the agreement.

fields.name: non_confusingly_similar_restriction
fields.std_description: Whether the licensee is prohibited from adopting confusingly similar designations.

fields.name: ownership_no_rights_acknowledgment
fields.std_description: Whether the licensee acknowledges it acquires no ownership rights in the licensed marks.

fields.name: party
fields.rlm_description: A party to the trademark license agreement.

fields.name: post_termination_use_rights
fields.std_description: Any rights or obligations regarding continued use or removal of the mark after termination.

fields.name: quality_standard
fields.std_description: The required quality level for goods bearing the licensed mark.

fields.name: schedule_reference
fields.rlm_description: A referenced schedule or attachment containing the relevant logo, specifications, or guidelines.

fields.name: termination_notice_requirement
fields.std_description: Whether termination requires written notice or may occur without notice.

fields.name: termination_right
fields.rlm_description: Indicates whether a party may terminate the agreement upon the stated trigger.

fields.name: termination_right_trigger
fields.std_description: The condition that allows Lucent to terminate the agreement or license.

fields.name: third_party_enforcement_obligation
fields.std_description: The licensee's duty to notify and cooperate regarding unauthorized third-party use of the marks.

fields.name: anti_boycott_compliance
fields.std_description: Indicates that the clause addresses antiboycott or anti-boycott prohibitions and related compliance.

fields.name: compliance_condition
fields.rlm_description: A stated condition that must be satisfied for export-related obligations to apply or continue.

fields.name: controlled_items
fields.rlm_description: Goods, technology, software, commodities, or other items subject to export control restrictions.

fields.name: cooperation_with_authorities
fields.std_description: Indicates that the parties must cooperate with each other or with governmental authorities to satisfy export compliance requirements.

fields.name: country_of_origin
fields.rlm_description: Declared country of origin for the goods or commodities.

fields.name: domestic_sale_with_buyer_export
fields.std_description: Indicates that the transaction is treated as a domestic sale unless the buyer later exports the product.

fields.name: economic_sanctions_compliance
fields.std_description: Indicates that the clause requires compliance with economic sanctions, embargo, or trade sanctions rules.

fields.name: export_classification_information
fields.std_description: Indicates that the clause requires disclosure or provision of export classification or origin information such as ECCN or HS codes.

fields.name: export_classification_number
fields.rlm_description: Export classification identifier for the controlled items, including ECCN or other export list numbers.

fields.name: export_control_compliance
fields.rlm_description: Whether the clause states that the party complies with export control, sanctions, embargo, and antiboycott requirements.

fields.name: export_control_compliance_obligation
fields.std_description: Indicates that a party must comply with applicable export control laws, regulations, or restrictions.

fields.name: export_control_section_number
fields.std_description: The section or clause number used for the export control provision.

fields.name: export_control_survival
fields.rlm_description: Whether the export control obligation survives termination or expiration of the agreement.

fields.name: export_permission_documents
fields.rlm_description: Specific documents or approvals required for export clearance or release.

fields.name: export_permission_obligation
fields.std_description: Indicates an obligation to obtain or assist in obtaining official export permissions, certificates, or declarations.

fields.name: export_permission_requirement
fields.rlm_description: Whether export, re-export, or release is conditioned on government license, permit, or other authorization.

fields.name: export_restriction_scope
fields.rlm_description: The export, re-export, transfer, shipment, or other activity restricted by the clause.

fields.name: fcpa_compliance
fields.std_description: Indicates that the clause requires compliance with the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act or similar anti-corruption laws.

fields.name: government_license_required
fields.std_description: Indicates that export activity requires prior government license, approval, or authorization.

fields.name: import_export_control_laws
fields.std_description: Indicates that the clause references compliance with import and/or export control laws in addition to export laws.

fields.name: issuing_authority
fields.rlm_description: The government body or authority referenced as granting, restricting, or enforcing export permissions.

fields.name: laws_and_regulations_referenced
fields.rlm_description: The statutes, regulations, and legal regimes cited as governing export control compliance.

fields.name: obligation_timing
fields.rlm_description: Any time period or timing language tied to the compliance or documentation obligation.

fields.name: prohibited_activity
fields.rlm_description: Specific acts expressly barred by the clause, such as unauthorized export, re-export, or boycott-related conduct.

fields.name: reexport_restriction
fields.std_description: Indicates a prohibition or restriction on exporting or re-exporting products, technology, or information without authorization.

fields.name: restricted_destination
fields.rlm_description: The prohibited country, jurisdiction, or recipient class referenced by the export restriction.

fields.name: restricted_party_prohibition
fields.std_description: Indicates a prohibition on exporting to restricted, proscribed, or sanctioned countries, persons, or entities.

fields.name: survival_after_termination
fields.std_description: Indicates that export control obligations survive termination or expiration of the agreement.

fields.name: wmd_end_use_restriction
fields.std_description: Indicates a restriction on exports to end-users engaged in weapons of mass destruction-related activities.

fields.name: additional_work_definition
fields.std_description: The contractual definition of additional work or additional services that qualify as a change order.

fields.name: change_management_plan
fields.rlm_description: The process used to accommodate changes to specifications or work.

fields.name: change_management_plan_reference
fields.std_description: Reference to a change management plan or similar governing process for handling changes.

fields.name: change_order
fields.rlm_description: A written instrument used to approve and document a change to the agreed work, services, deliverables, or specifications.

fields.name: change_order_alternative_terms
fields.std_description: Alternative labels used for the same or similar change-order concept in the agreement.

fields.name: change_order_definition
fields.std_description: The contractual definition of what constitutes a change order or change request.

fields.name: change_order_effective_condition
fields.std_description: The condition under which the change order becomes binding or performance may begin.

fields.name: delivery_schedule
fields.rlm_description: The delivery timing schedule that may be impacted by a change order.

fields.name: ecr
fields.rlm_description: An engineering change request issued by a party or contractor.

fields.name: fee_or_price_adjustment
fields.std_description: Whether the change order may result in an added cost, fee change, or contract price adjustment.

fields.name: mutual_written_execution_required
fields.std_description: Whether both parties must sign or mutually agree in writing for the change order to be effective.

fields.name: schedule_impact
fields.std_description: Whether the change order may affect delivery timing, milestones, or completion dates.

fields.name: scope
fields.rlm_description: The work description or service scope set out in the written agreement or change order.

fields.name: scope_of_work
fields.rlm_description: The agreed work definition that a change may alter, amend, enhance, add to, or delete from.

fields.name: scope_of_work_change_trigger
fields.std_description: The type of change that triggers the need for a change order, typically changes outside the agreed scope or specifications.

fields.name: services
fields.rlm_description: The services that may be added, altered, amended, enhanced, or deleted through a change order.

fields.name: specifications
fields.rlm_description: The technical or functional specifications that a change management plan is intended to accommodate.

fields.name: work
fields.rlm_description: The work set forth in the change management plan reference.

fields.name: written_approval_requirement
fields.std_description: Whether the change order must be submitted, approved, executed, or otherwise agreed to in writing before work proceeds.

fields.name: certificate_as_representation
fields.std_description: Indicates whether certificates signed by officers are deemed to be representations and warranties under the agreement.

fields.name: clause_title
fields.std_description: The heading naming the representations and warranties section.

fields.name: counterparty_recipient
fields.rlm_description: The party to whom the representations and warranties are made.

fields.name: joint_and_several
fields.std_description: Indicates whether the warranting parties are jointly and severally bound.

fields.name: joint_and_several_liability
fields.rlm_description: Indicates whether the representing parties are jointly and severally bound by the representations and warranties.

fields.name: recipient_party
fields.std_description: The party or parties to whom the representations and warranties are made.

fields.name: representation_intro_phrase
fields.std_description: The standard introductory wording used to begin the representations and warranties section.

fields.name: representing_parties
fields.rlm_description: The parties that jointly and severally make the representations and warranties in the clause.

fields.name: section_number
fields.std_description: The article or section identifier introducing the representations and warranties clause.

fields.name: warranting_parties
fields.std_description: The party or parties making the representations and warranties.

fields.name: commencement_date
fields.std_description: The commencement date used as the starting point for some contract-year definitions or related periods.
fields.rlm_description: The date on which a defined period or obligation commences.

fields.name: contract_period
fields.rlm_description: The overall term of the agreement, usually expressed as a start and end date or a defined period.

fields.name: contract_period_end_date
fields.std_description: The date on which the contract period ends, subject to earlier termination if stated.
fields.rlm_description: The date on which the contract period ends.

fields.name: contract_period_start_date
fields.std_description: The date on which the contract period begins.
fields.rlm_description: The date on which the contract period begins.

fields.name: contract_territory
fields.rlm_description: The geographic territory covered by the agreement or defined term.

fields.name: contract_year
fields.rlm_description: A recurring annual or twelve-month period defined within the contract term.

fields.name: contract_year_definition
fields.std_description: The length and boundary rule used to define a contract year.

fields.name: contract_year_end_date
fields.std_description: The date on which a specific contract year ends.
fields.rlm_description: The date on which a specific contract year ends.

fields.name: contract_year_number
fields.std_description: A numbered contract year referenced in schedules or definitions.
fields.rlm_description: The numbered contract year referenced in the agreement.

fields.name: contract_year_quarter
fields.rlm_description: A three-month subperiod occurring during a contract year.

fields.name: contract_year_quarter_definition
fields.std_description: The length and boundary rule used to define a contract year quarter.

fields.name: contract_year_start_date
fields.rlm_description: The date on which a specific contract year begins.

fields.name: contract_year_start_rule
fields.std_description: The rule or anchor date used to start each contract year.

fields.name: definition_text
fields.rlm_description: The full textual definition assigned to the term, including qualifying language and cross-references when present.

fields.name: duration_length
fields.rlm_description: The length of a defined period when expressed as a recurring interval.

fields.name: effective_date
fields.std_description: The effective date used as the starting point for some contract-year definitions.
fields.rlm_description: The date from which a defined term or period begins to run.

fields.name: term_end_date
fields.std_description: The final date of the overall term when a clause ties the last contract year to the term end.

fields.name: term_name
fields.rlm_description: The defined contract term or phrase being introduced in the clause.

fields.name: time_of_day
fields.rlm_description: A specified clock time used to define when a period begins or ends.
